अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भ‍वत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भ‍क्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥ २३ ॥
sanskrit_iast: antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁtad bhavaty alpa-medhasāmdevān deva-yajo yāntimad-bhaktā yānti mām api

anvaya.meaning: medhasām — of those of small intelligence
yajaḥ — the worshipers of the demigods

translations.verbatim_quote: Men of small intelligence worship the demigods, and their fruits are limited and temporary. Those who worship the demigods go to the planets of the demigods, but My devotees ultimately reach My supreme planet.

Men of small intelligence worship the demigods, and their fruits are limited and temporary. Those who worship the demigods go to the planets of the demigods, but My devotees ultimately reach My supreme planet.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2837 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Some commentators on the Bhagavad-gītā say that one who worships a demigod can reach the Supreme Lord, but here it is clearly stated that the worshipers of demigods go to the different planetary systems where various demigods are situated, just as a worshiper of the sun achieves the sun or a worship

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.23 Since those non-discriminating men with desires are engaged in disciplines for limited results, therefore, tat phalam, that result; tesam, of theirs; alpamedhasam, who are of poor intellect, of poor wisdom; antavat tu bhavati, is limited, ephemeral, indeed.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-23-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: The fruit of the non-discriminating (alpa-medhasām) who pursue limited disciplines is itself bounded (antavat); worshippers of gods go to those gods; Krishna's devotees come to Krishna alone. The structural critique is the mismatch between effort-expended and the boundedness of the chosen form.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.23 The men of ‘small understanding’ means those whose understanding is poor, who worship only Indra and other divinities. The fruit of their worship is small and finite. Why? The worshippers of divinities like Indra go to the divinities.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-23-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Worshippers of limited divinities go to those divinities; the divinities possess limited joy and live only for a limited time; the worshippers, attaining equality with them, also fall down with them in due course. Krishna's devotees, knowing their acts as Krishna's worship, come to Krishna and abide.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All readings agree the fruit is bounded (antavat) when the worship is bounded. Shankara emphasises the structural critique (poor discrimination yokes effort to bounded forms); Ramanuja emphasises the boundedness of the divinity itself (limited joy, limited duration). The engineering analog reflects what all readings share — bounded-target practice yields bounded result — and stays at operational scope (commitment to surface-techniques returns surface-results; commitment to substrate returns substrate-results).

literal_meaning: But the fruit of those of small intelligence is finite; worshippers of gods go to the gods, my devotees come to me.
traditional_meaning_consensus: antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām / devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api — the fruit of those poor-in-discrimination is bounded; worshippers of (limited) deities go to those deities (and share their bounded duration), Krishna's devotees come to Krishna himself. Shankara: alpa-medhasām — those of poor discrimination — undertake the same effort but for limited results; the structural critique is that effort-yoked-to-bounded-form yields bounded fruit. Ramanuja: the divinities have limited joy and limited duration; identification with them inherits the duration; Krishna's devotees alone reach the unbounded.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
